channel.title: Soldiers of the Cross - World Ministries International

item.title: Urgent Compassion for Malawi
item.content:encoded:
Dear Soldiers of The Cross Intercessor:

I have converted my wife's article into a SOC letter for your deep prayers.

We leave for South Africa and then onto Malawi on August 31 returning October 6, 2026. In order to help widows and orphans in Malawi we need people to help us not only by prayers but by giving. Most people do not have the opportunity to travel as they are committed in work or they just are not able. Some would not want to go as it is always a level of danger of violence and disease as it is one of the poorest countries on earth. Even though you cannot go or don’t want to go, you can still be a part of fulfilling God's commandments to help the poor, widows and orphans by sending us with your prayers and finances. Please intercede that all who gets this article or listens to this need on radio, television or social media will give something.

Please consider this important matter in your prayers. We could use thousands of dollars to help the poor in Malawi. We still need $6,462 for airline tickets and accommodations etc., as well as $5,875 to travel etc., for South Africa. Please, we need these funds immediately to purchase all tickets.

Please click on the link below to make your donations to The Dorcas Fund, the South Africa trip and the Malawi trip.

https://www.worldministries.org/donate.aspx

Now please read my wife's exciting article below.

Blessings, Dr. Jonathan Hansen

Turning Compassion into Action

The Dorcas Fund was founded from a deeply personal promise to God.

As a child, I experienced loss, rejection, and trauma. I understand what it means for a child to feel vulnerable, unseen, or uncertain about the future. Through the grace of God, my pain did not become the end of my story. It became part of the reason I now feel called to serve children and families facing hardship.

I made a vow to the Lord that whenever He gave me the ability and opportunity to help, I would do my best to support orphans, traumatized children, widows, and families in need.

That promise became The Dorcas Fund.

Under the umbrella of World Ministries International, the Dorcas Fund exists to provide practical care with dignity and compassion. Our work has reached children and families in Kenya, the United States, Philippines and Israel. Now we are preparing to serve in Malawi.

This September, we plan to visit two orphanages and a village in Malawi. Our goal is to provide essential items that can make an immediate difference in daily life; including food supplies, clothing, sanitary products, mattresses, bedding, and other basic necessities.

The heart of the Dorcas Fund is not simply to give things. It is to remind people that they are seen, valued, and not forgotten.

We especially desire to reach children who have lost their parents, children living in single-parent households, children affected by trauma, and those who have been abandoned, rejected, or overlooked because of disability or poverty.

Every contribution, whether large or small, helps us continue this work. A donation can help place food on a table, provide a child with clean clothing, give a girl access to sanitary supplies, or allow a child to sleep on a proper mattress instead of the floor.

We invite individuals, churches, organizations, and friends of the ministry to partner with us in this Malawi initiative.

To give, please visit: www.worldministries.org and click on The Dorcas Fund tab, you can also call our office at (360) 629-5248 and use your credit/debit card or send a check or money order to World Ministries Int'l, PO Box 277, Stanwood, WA 98292 and designate your donation for The Dorcas Fund.

Together, we can turn compassion into practical action and bring hope to children who need to know that their lives matter.


Dr. Adalia Hansen
Founder, The Dorcas Fund
World Ministries International

item.title: The Sacrifice God Desires
item.content:encoded:
“And to love Him with all the heart, with all the understanding, with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love one’s neighbor as oneself, is more than all the whole burnt offerings and sacrifices." — Mark 12:33 (AMP)

As intercessors, we understand sacrifice. We sacrifice our time in prayer, our sleep in night watches, our comfort in fasting, and often our finances in supporting the work of the Kingdom. We offer praise when we do not feel like praising and worship when circumstances are difficult. These sacrifices are precious before God.

Yet Jesus reminds us of something even greater.

In Mark 12:33, He declares that loving God with all our heart, understanding, soul, and strength, and loving our neighbor as ourselves, is more important than all burnt offerings and sacrifices.

This means that before God looks at the sacrifice, He looks at the heart offering it.

We may pray for nations, ministries, churches, leaders, and families, but if our hearts carry prejudice, bitterness, hatred, resentment, pride, or partiality, then we have missed the very foundation upon which true intercession is built. God desires not only prayers from our lips but love flowing from our hearts.

The Lord is not merely seeking intercessors who pray long prayers; He is seeking sons and daughters who love Him completely and reflect His love toward others. Our prayers gain greater weight when they flow from a heart surrendered to God and purified by His love.

Let us continually ask the Holy Spirit to search our hearts. Are we carrying unforgiveness? Do we have hatred toward certain people, races, tribes, denominations, or social classes? Are we praying for others while secretly harboring resentment toward them?

The greatest sacrifice is not merely what we give up; it is giving God our whole heart.

May our prayers, worship, fasting, and service be rooted in genuine love for God and sincere love for our neighbor. Then our sacrifices will rise before Him as a pleasing offering, flowing from a heart that reflects His own.

We appreciate all that you do. We are looking forward to the ministry work still left for this year. Pray for support that we will accomplish each mission.

Blessings,

Dr. Adalia Hansen

Dear Prayer Partner,

  1. I want to thank you for your prayers as the $1,700 needed to travel to Prince Rupert, Canada on August 6-10 has been received in full. Great answer to prayer!
  1. Also, $1,935 has come in for the trip to Malawi with $8,065 still needing to come in.
  1. $582.72 was sent in for Zambia with a balance of $9,417.28 still needing to come in for travel to Zambia.
  1. We received $1,062.89 for Korea. We still need $4,937.11 to minister in Korea.
  1. We are still in need of $10,000 each for Kenya, Liberia, Tanzania, and Israel, as well as $9,000 for South Africa.
  1. The balance we need to travel and minister in the 8 remaining countries is $71,419.39.

Please earnestly pray with us that the remaining finances will come in so we can help the bride of Christ in these nations as well as the poor people that needs food, clothing, shoes and other necessities.

Blessings,

Dr. Jonathan Hansen

item.title: PRAYER NEEDS
item.content:encoded:
Dear Prayer Partner,

My wife, Dr. Adalia Hansen and I have some serious needs that we need intercessory prayer on so that we can have critical breakthroughs. Without these breakthroughs we cannot travel to and minister in nine different nations.

  1. We immediately need $1,700 USD to travel to Prince Rupert Canada on August 6-10.

  2. We need to travel and minister in Kenya, Liberia, Malawi, Zambia, and Tanzania at a cost of $10,000 USD apiece or $50,000 USD approximately for all five African nations combined.

  3. Finally, this year I need to travel to South Korea at a cost of $6,000 USD, Israel at $10,000 USD and South Africa at $9,000 USD for an additional total for these three nations of $25,000 USD.

May God richly bless you as you help us pray these urgent needs into existence so we can travel to these nations with the Word of God as well as meeting some other critical needs people have in order to survive!

Blessings and Thank you,

Drs. Jonathan & Adalia Hansen

item.title: Dear Soldiers of the Cross and faithful Intercessors
item.content:encoded:
We give thanks to God for His covering over our recent mission to Kenya. Through your prayers, the trip was not only successful but deeply impactful. We held multiple gatherings, including a powerful women’s conference, Sunday services, and private ministry sessions. In every setting, the presence of God was undeniable, bringing deliverance, healing, and breakthrough to many lives.

We witnessed doors opening beyond what we expected, even into other nations. Truly, your prayers carried us and we are grateful for your unwavering spiritual support.

As we look ahead, we are preparing for missions in Malawi and Tanzania in due time this year. We humbly ask you to continue standing with us in prayer for divine provision, for partners, and for laborers willing to go into the field.

Through our Dorcas ministry, we are believing God to extend practical love to communities in need. Our vision includes supporting widows and orphans, providing clothing, food, and basic necessities such as sanitary pads, etc., and empowering women by helping them start small businesses. We desire to bring both spiritual and tangible transformation to these villages and communities.

We ask that you pray with us for helpers, resources, and hearts willing to join this mission. May God connect us with those who will hold our hands in this work, so that together we may serve and uplift those in need.

Thank you for standing with us. Your prayers are not in vain.

With gratitude and faith, Dr. Adalia Hansen

Dear Precious Prayer Warrior:

  1. We thank God for His provisions or we could not travel the nations.
  2. We need to travel to Tanzania and Malawi soon, as we have been requested to go to both. The needs in both of these African countries are enormous. As soon as we receive $10,000 US for these countries, we will buy the tickets and go. We will go to whichever country the finances we receive first, followed by the second country.
  3. Zambia is on the horizon as God is opening the doors and the Holy Spirit is confirming in 2026, we will also labor there for the Lord. If we receive a donation of $30,000 for those three countries, we will immediately purchase our tickets and put together the itinerary for these nations.
  4. Time is racing by and the New World Order is already strategizing to control these nations. Only the church can stop it with proper leadership leading a National Repentance. Help me to meet with the leadership of both, church and government, to come under the authority of Jesus Christ and lead a National Repentance.
  5. Thank you for your prayers and your financial response to these requests as I know people do pray and one or more of you give to these critical requests and special needs.

Blessings, Dr. Jonathan Hansen

item.title: The Role of True Intercessors in Revival
item.content:encoded:
True intercessors are not just ordinary individuals; they are vetted, broken people who God has authorized to stand in the gap. They act as the backbone of revival, transcending personal prayer to carry divine burdens for nations. This calling requires high levels of spiritual maturity, humility, and a deep alignment with the Holy Spirit.

Intercessors are more than mere prayer warriors; they are authorized mediators who hold a significant position within the priesthood. Through fervent prayer, they can shift systems and laws, influencing the spiritual landscape and facilitating divine interventions.

One of the fundamental attributes of a true intercessor is deep brokenness and humility. God entrusts this intense spiritual authority only to those who exhibit a genuine posture of humility. In a world where arrogance can often overshadow spiritual service, intercessors demonstrate the power of humility, recognizing that their strength lies in their weakness.

True intercessors are propelled by divine burdens rather than rigid schedules. They often feel the weight of God's heart for a city, nation, or individual. This profound sense of responsibility drives them to petition fervently, standing tirelessly on behalf of others. Their intercession actively dismantles demonic installations, holding territories in darkness, making them targets for spiritual attacks on their finances, health, and lives.

As intercessors grow in their calling, they often evolve into “watchers", or keepers of God's jealousy. In this role, they gain the authority to discern spiritual conditions and, at times, execute spiritual judgment. This evolution signifies not just growth in authority but also a deeper understanding of their responsibilities.

A vital aspect of effective intercession is a full understanding of one's identity in Christ, firmly rooted in justification, and the transformative power of the Holy Spirit. This knowledge is crucial to avoid dangerous and ineffective prayer practices. It empowers intercessors to engage with confidence and discernment in their spiritual battles.

The call to intercession is a profound and sacred responsibility. True intercessors embody humility, authority, and a deep connection to God's heart. By standing in the gap, they play a pivotal role in ushering in revival and bringing about transformative change within their communities and beyond.

Thank you for standing in the gap on our behalf. Continually commit all things to the Lord, protection, provision and growth.

Blessings,
Dr. Adalia Hansen

The Responsibility of Believers

  1. Please intercede as we need $10,000 USD to be in Kenya for the month of April. This will provide our airfares, traveling expenses, and give us some money to help widows and orphans as well as others in desperate need. I also need prayer for funds to pay for housing in addition to this $10,000.
  2. Pray for our meetings in Kenya with leadership and churches as we call for a National Repentance.
  3. Pray for my wife as she will also be speaking in venues including a National Women’s Conference.
  4. Pray for breakthroughs in our finances so we can do much more for God’s Kingdom expansion.
  5. Pray that many more people will join our Soldiers of the Cross Intercessor team by clicking on the link to join or call our office at (360) 629-5248.

Sincerely,
Dr. Jonathan Hansen

item.title: ENGAGING IN SPIRITUAL WARFARE
item.content:encoded:
Dear Intercessors,

Grasping the nature of spiritual battles is essential for effective engagement in intercession and prayer. Spiritual battles are fought in an unseen realm. The struggle is not only about visible issues but also about underlying spiritual influences. It is crucial to recognize that people are not the enemy; the battle is against spiritual wickedness in heavenly places.

Ephesians 6:12 highlights that we wrestle not against flesh and blood but against spiritual powers and forces. Understanding that demonic entities aim to thwart God’s purposes and can influence thoughts, emotions, and actions. These may include deception, division, fear, and discouragement, which can lead believers away from their purpose.

Every battle has eternal implications, affecting not just individual lives but also communities and even nations. Developing the ability to identify signs of spiritual warfare, such as increased conflict or unusual challenges, can be crucial for preparation. You walk into this ability by being sensitive to the Holy Spirit. While it involves standing firm against attacks (defensive), it also requires actively fighting for truth and resisting deception (offensive).

Understand that you are fighting from a place of victory (Jesus has already won) rather than for victory, applying that truth to brokenness.

Effective warfare requires humility, submission to God, and reliance on His strength rather than your own.

The overarching goal of spiritual warfare is to expand the Kingdom of God and reveal His glory, making intercessors aware that their prayers can lead to divine intervention and transformation. Being attuned to the Spirit’s guidance helps you discern the specific areas to focus on and the nature of the battle.

Continually pray for the ministry to overcome and thrive in the places that God has called it.

Blessings, Pastor Adalia Hansen

Dear Prayer Warriors,

  1. We have a budget of $23,000 a month and have not been reaching it. Consequently, my wife and I have been sacrificing for others so they can get paid. Please pray this victory in so there is plenty in the store house and all can be blessed.
  1. We need more loyal staff. Pray that those who are self sufficient or get a retirement check come to us so we don’t have to pay any more salary.
  1. Keep praying for God’s favor, anointing, and protection over my family and the ministry here.

Blessings, Dr. Jonathan Hansen

item.title: Intercession as a Calling
item.content:encoded:
In the Bible, intercession is presented as both a general call for all believers and a specific, Spirit-given calling or gift for some individuals. All Christians are urged to pray for one another, for leaders, and for the world. (1 Thessalonians 5:17) However, some feel a profound, consistent, and specific burden to stand in the gap through dedicated prayer, which many interpret as a special calling. (Ezekiel 22:30)

Intercession flows from revelation to routine. It requires death to self, which is why it cannot be assigned casually; it must be called.

Characteristics of an Intercessor's Calling

Those with a specific calling to intercession often exhibit certain traits:

  • A deep compassion and love for others.
  • A profound sensitivity to the spiritual realm and the leading of the Holy Spirit.
  • An ability to bear spiritual burdens for extended periods, sometimes physically feeling the pain or distress of the person or situation they are praying for.
  • Persistence in prayer, refusing to give up until God's will is manifest.
  • A strong belief that God moves in direct response to their prayers.

The calling of an intercessor is a vital, behind-the-scenes ministry that undergirds the entire church and its mission, partnering with God to see His will accomplished on earth. It is a calling etched into the spirit. It is the labor room of heaven.

You stood in the gap when it was unseen and costly. You carried burdens heaven assigned. You prayed, mercy prevailed and doors shifted. Your prayers mattered more than you will ever know. We honor you and thank God for you.

Blessings,
Dr. Adalia Hansen

Dear Fellow Prayer Warrior:

We desperately need your prayers for our debt of roughly $39,000 and that people will call in or go to our web site to donate. We had a tremendous year but we need to end well with no debt entering 2026.

Pray that each person receiving our bi-monthly newsletters, as well as our intercessors, will give a good year-end donation. Remember, all donations are tax deductible.

Thank you for your prayers for our Philippines and WA, DC trips. Both were very successful.

Pray that we will add many new intercessors so we can be more productive for Jesus.

I just received a promise from a donor who will match gifts until the amount reaches $5,000, so please give generously and pray everyone else will do the same!


Blessings, Dr. Jonathan Hansen

item.title: The Forgotten Faces - SOC NL
item.content:encoded:
Across the world, millions of orphaned children wake up each day carrying an invisible burden; the weight of being unwanted, unseen, and unloved. They live among us, yet they are treated as though they don’t belong to the human life story. Their pain is silent, their existence often overlooked, and their humanity too easily dismissed.

They walk among us, unseen. They sit beside us, unnoticed. They live beneath roofs of neglect and sleep under skies that offer no comfort. In almost every society, they are looked down upon, pitied but rarely embraced. They are treated as burdens rather than blessings, as inconveniences rather than human beings. Many are called names that pierce deeper than knives; “bastard," “child of nobody," “the one with no name." The world reminds them of what they lost, but seldom reminds them of what they’re worth.

As children, they grow up with questions that few can answer. They wonder if love was ever meant for them, or if they were born only to be abandoned. Their hearts harden not out of choice, but out of survival. They learn early that to cry is useless, because no one comes running. Many are mistreated, abused, and made to feel subhuman. They are sent to work instead of school. They are given leftovers instead of meals. They are told to “be grateful" even when all they’ve known is rejection. Some are shuffled between relatives who see them as free labor, or institutions where love is rationed like food, where they are fed but not held, clothed but not comforted. They learn early that no one comes when they cry, and that to survive, they must silence their hearts. As they grow into adults, the stigma follows. Even in marriage, they are sometimes unwanted. Families whisper, “She has no roots… he’s a child of nobody." An orphan woman marries and is told she’s “lucky" someone accepted her. An orphan man succeeds in life, yet his achievements are minimized because “he came from nowhere." They fight to prove their worth in a world that decided their value long before they could speak. They are denied belonging and the dignity that others receive freely.

In the Philippines, there are young girls who sell their bodies for as little as $17 an hour to tourists, not because they want to, but because survival has no pride. In Kenya, it can be as little as 100 shillings at the Coast of Mombasa, less than a dollar, just to find food for one night. Across Africa and South America, young boys roam the streets, hungry and afraid. Some sell their innocence to predators who promise them a meal. Others are pulled into drug trades, gangs, or same-sex exploitation, just to survive another day.

This is the dark underworld that polite society refuses to see. These are the children Jesus spoke of when He said, “Whatever you did to the least of these, you did unto Me." The world, wrapped in its comfort, scrolls past their stories as if they are too distant to matter.

Many of these orphans grow up questioning even God Himself. They wonder, “If He is a Father to the fatherless, why did He not choose me?" Their faith becomes fragile. Their hope, dim. Some stop believing that anyone, even God, could truly love them. Yet in their silence, Heaven still hears their cry. The same Jesus who was rejected, abandoned, and hung naked on a cross, identifies with them. He knows what it means to be despised, to be without shelter and to be misunderstood.

That is where we come in. If we say we carry the Spirit of Christ, we cannot ignore what breaks His heart. The Gospel is not only about preaching; it is about embodying love to the forgotten. It is about being the hands, the feet, and the embrace of Jesus to those who believe they are invisible.

The truth is uncomfortable, but comfort has never changed the world, compassion has. If we will not stand up for the orphans, who will? If the Church will not love them, who will show them Christ? If we will not feed, clothe, and shelter them, then our gospel is only theory not truth.

This is not just about charity; it is about justice. It is about restoring dignity to the sons and daughters that society has labeled “nobody."

I write this with a burdened heart, not to evoke pity, but to awaken conscience. The need is real. The pain is real. The cry is still echoing. We can no longer be silent.

If you feel moved to help, you can be part of this redemptive work. Every gift, every act of love, every dollar given brings hope to those who have forgotten how to dream. Together, we can remind them that they are not forgotten, not by us, and certainly not by God. Let us build homes of hope. Let us feed hungry mouths and heal broken hearts. Let us rewrite their story, because Christ never forgot them, and neither should we.

We are scheduled to travel to the Philippines on November 19th on a mission trip, not only to speak but also provide benevolence aid. If you wish to contribute to our efforts in supporting the under-privileged, donations are very much welcomed.

Blessings

Dr. Adalia Hansen
Founder of The Dorcas Fund

Dear Intercessors,

  1. We are in the Philippines, helping to restore hope & lives. Not only speaking the gospel to the nation but helping widows, orphans, poor people that are suffering, starving, & thousands that are flooded out of their homes, etc. Today is Thanksgiving & I will be at the prison in the Philippines, ministering to the prisoners, giving them hope.
  2. Please, please help us so we can end the year not as much in debt as we are right now, we are lacking probably over $50,000. We really need your help. Do what you can to help us end the year without a loss.
  3. On Tuesday, November 25th, we rode in canoes to minister & give food to the flood victims.

    May God richly bless you & Happy Thanksgiving!
    Dr. Jonathan Hansen

item.title: Philippines
item.content:encoded:
Dear Soldiers of the Cross,

Over the past year, the Lord has taken us on an extraordinary journey each month — including South Korea, Israel, 4 trips to Kenya, Malawi, Norway, Canada, Washington D.C., Mar a Lago Florida, Baltimore Maryland and different cities in Washington State. In every place, His hand of protection and favor has covered us. We give all glory to God.

Now, as we prepare for our upcoming mission to the Philippines, we are asking our intercessors and prayer partners to stand with us once again. During this trip, we will not only be ministering the Word in churches and to local ministers and congregations, but also carrying out a benevolence outreach with Dr. Hyodo and myself as part of my ongoing Dorcas Fund initiative, bringing relief and hope to the poor in Manila.

We are asking for focused prayer in the following areas:

  1. Provision and Finances: Pray for a complete financial breakthrough for this trip — for the mission work itself, for the benevolence outreach, and for backup resources under the Dorcas Fund. May there be more than enough to meet every need.
  2. Spiritual Sensitivity: Pray that we will minister from a place of deep hearing — that we will carry the right word from the heart of God for every church, minister, congregation, and even for the nation of the Philippines.
  3. Divine Protection and Accomplishment: Pray for covering and safety in all travel and ministry activities. May the Lord’s hand be upon us from departure to return, and may every purpose He has ordained be fulfilled without hindrance.
  4. Intercession for the United States and the Body of Christ: As we go, please continue to lift up the nation of America and the Church. There is much division and spiritual confusion in this hour. Pray that the voice of the Holy Spirit will rise clearly above every noise, leading the people of God to choose His way, and not to be swayed by the winds of deception or the seeds the enemy has planted within the body. May unity, repentance, and discernment return to the Church.

We deeply appreciate every one of you who stands with us through prayer and giving. Your partnership is not in vain. Together we are advancing the Kingdom and carrying the light of Christ into the nations.

With love and gratitude,

Dr. Adalia Hansen
Founder of Dorcas Women and Children’s Fund
Vice President, World Ministries International
Email: ahansen.wmi@hotmail.com

Dear Intercessor:

We seriously need your intercession for our finances. We cannot go on these monthly trips, sounding the alarm and giving warning to leaders of nations without enough finances. We are short financially on many trips and cannot continue this way without coming to a complete stop. We need at least $20,000 to catch up on mission trip debts.

Please pray for Terri Pulley, who has been with me since 1998. She recently lost her son who died suddenly from a cardiac arrest. He had a wife and three children.

Also, I have another invitation from The Israel Allies Foundation to go and honor President Donald Trump in WA, D.C. in December. This would cost about $10,000, if the Lord is leading you to help me take this trip.

Blessings, Dr. Jonathan Hansen

item.title: WHOM SHALL WE SEND?
item.content:encoded:
Dear Intercessors,

When Isaiah stood before the throne of God, he was undone by holiness, convicted by his unclean lips, and yet purified by the coal of mercy.

It was only after this encounter that he heard the cry of heaven: “Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?" That cry has never been silenced. It still reverberates across nations, across generations, across the hidden places of our hearts. God is still looking for a man, a woman, a vessel who will rise above excuses and say yes.

To be sent is not a light call, it is a surrender of self, a release of comfort, and a laying down of every ambition for the sake of His kingdom. It is to become the hands and voice of Christ in a hurting world, the one who carries His message of hope where despair reigns. Today, heaven is still asking, who will go?

Will we remain silent, waiting for another, or will we bow low and lift our voice as Isaiah did, saying, “Here am I, send me"?

Let this be our prayer: Lord, purge me of pride and fear. Make me a willing vessel. Use me in my home, in my generation, and in my nation. Do not pass me by when You are searching for one to send. May my life be an answer to the cry of heaven, until every corner of the earth has heard that Jesus still saves.

Adalia Hansen

Dear Warrior Intercessor:

Our mission travel schedule continues to be very busy through the end of the year. As you can imagine, this constant traveling can be very challenging physically, so we are calling on our Soldiers of the Cross Intercessor team as well as our faithful financial partners to hold us in prayer over the next few months. On September 29th, a team of eight, including my family, join me as we travel to South Korea for the World Holy Spirit Conference. We covet your prayers for God's protection over the team and that God's will be accomplished during our time there. When we return on October 5th, I have a brief rest before I go to Canada for a conference on October 9th - 11th. Then I will be attending a global conference in Baltimore, MD on October 17th. Following that, I will leave for Israel to attend the Knesset Christian Allies Caucus (KCAC) in Jerusalem Israel in their parliament (KCAC) meeting with Ambassador Mike Huckabee on October 21, 2025. All these mission trips are so critical at this time and carry with them a tremendous financial burden. We are asking each of you to be in prayer about what you might be able to contribute to help us cover the expense of airfare and accommodations necessary to make these trips possible. Time is short and we need to boldly speak truth to leaders. May God bless you all as you continue to stand with us.

Blessings, Jonathan Hansen

item.title: PRAYER FOR HEALING
item.content:encoded:
Dear Intercessors,

The Church serves as a living testament to His love, unity, and power in the world. However, it is not immune to suffering wounds. Today, many believers enter sanctuaries with smiles on their faces while concealing hidden wounds of rejection, betrayal, burnout, abuse, loneliness, grief, and unspoken disappointments. Even leaders are not spared, as pastors often guide their flock with broken hearts, carrying burdens they cannot disclose.

If left unaddressed, these wounds seep into the fabric of the Church, breeding division, offense, distrust, and spiritual stagnation. The adversary understands that a wounded soldier is more easily subdued than a resilient one; hence he targets the heart of the Church, its people.

Yet, we give thanks to God, for we serve the ultimate Healer. Jesus proclaimed that the Spirit of the Lord had anointed Him to mend the brokenhearted. It is the duty of intercessors to stand in the gap and invoke the healing touch of the Spirit upon the Church. Where there is bitterness, we proclaim forgiveness. Where there is despair, we speak of hope. Where there is weariness, we cry out for rejuvenation.

As intercessors, this month let us pray for:

  1. Upcoming Trips - We have 6 to 7 trips remaining this year: Kenya, Malawi, South Korea, Israel, the Philippines, and Kenya again. Also, we might try and squeeze in Liberia. Kindly seek the wisdom of God in all these endeavors.
  2. Continual prayers for financial backing from partners and individuals inspired to contribute to the vision.
  3. Prayers for our staff, who are invaluable to us, for their health and well-being.
  4. Healing for the wounded hearts of ministers and the body of Christ. May the oil of the Holy Spirit soothe the concealed pains, disappointments, and grief among believers.

"Lord, heal Your Church. Bind every broken heart. Let the essence of Christ's love flow once more through the Body, so that the world may recognize that we belong to You. Amen."

Blessings, Adalia Hansen

Dear Intercessor Warrior Partner:

We really need your prayers as we have lost some monthly financial partners due to illness, death or their husbands don’t want them to give. We need a real breakthrough. We have had to cancel one television contract due to a lack of finances.

Also, we need to go on the trips my wife mentioned above to meet with the government and church leaders to exhort them to call for a national repentance as well as warn them of the intentions of the New World Order to control their nation and lives. Please support us earnestly in your prayers and finances. Time is short to sound the alarm and save lives.

In Jesus name, Jonathan Hansen

item.title: Fueling the Mission through Prayer
item.content:encoded:
Dear Intercessors,

Thank you for standing with us in prayer. Even though we don’t meet in person, please know how deeply your prayers are felt and appreciated. What you do behind the scenes matters more than words can express.

We know that ministry doesn’t move forward on effort alone, it moves on prayer. Your faithfulness to cover us is part of the reason doors open, lives are touched, and God continues to show up in powerful ways.

This week, we ask that you continue lifting us up in these specific areas:

  • Wisdom and clarity in key decisions we need to make
  • Strength and health for our team spiritually, emotionally, and physically
  • Protection against discouragement and spiritual attack

We may not see each other, but we are deeply connected by purpose. Thank you for praying when no one is watching, for standing in the gap, and for helping carry the weight of this call.

With gratitude,

item.title: Financial Harvest andamp; Fulfillment of Prophetic Promises
item.content:encoded:
Dear Intercessors,

I am writing to express our gratitude for your unwavering support and faithful prayers for World Ministries International. Your intercession has sustained us in ways that words cannot express.

Though we are witnessing national and global shakings, we are also seeing God place us in rooms and before leaders we never imagined we’d stand with. This, we know, is the hand of God and the fruit of prophetic promises spoken over this ministry. Yet, despite these divine appointments, we are not seeing the fruit thereof. While our calling and vision continue to grow, the financial provision needed to carry it forward has not yet matched what God has spoken.

We believe God is not a man that He should lie (Numbers 23:19). The words spoken over this ministry, words of abundant provision, supernatural favor, and sustained impact, were not empty. We are asking you, our intercessory family, to help us press in until we see the full manifestation.

We’ve recently experienced loss within the ministry. One of our volunteer staff members, April Marlow, who was an integral part of our staff fellowship, is no longer with us. She helped us carry the weight of the work behind the scenes. Her absence has created a tangible gap, emotionally and spiritually. Please keep her family in prayer.

We ask for your prayers in the following areas:

  • Supernatural financial release—that what is ours will no longer be delayed or withheld.
  • Manifestation of every prophetic word related to resources, staffing, partnerships, and global impact.
  • Divine connections and strategic alliances to move the ministry forward in power and integrity.
  • Encouragement and strength for the team to endure and keep building in faith.
  • For Rhonda Marlow and family over the loss of her daughter/sister.
  • Fresh wind of volunteers and staff who are aligned with the mission and heartbeat of this work.

We are standing on Galatians 6:9 (NIV) – “Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up."

We believe that now is the set time for favor (Psalm 102:13), and we are trusting God to release what has been declared. Thank you for standing with us at this critical moment. If the Lord speaks anything to you in prayer, please share it; we are listening and discerning.

With love, faith, and deep appreciation, Adalia Hansen

Please pray earnestly about these needs and our financial obligations for these trips.

item.title: Staying Spiritually Healthy
item.content:encoded:
Luke 5:16 (NIV) But Jesus often withdrew to lonely places and prayed...

As intercessors, we carry deep burdens in the spirit, sometimes for people we’ve never met, nations we’ve never visited, and situations we may never see resolved with our own eyes. The call is weighty, but so is the importance of staying spiritually healthy while doing this work. Jesus, our perfect example, often withdrew to lonely places, not just to war in prayer, but to be with the Father, to rest, and to be renewed. If He needed that rhythm, so do we.

One of the greatest dangers for intercessors is spiritual fatigue. It doesn’t always show up as tiredness. It can come in the form of emotional numbness, irritability, a lack of clarity in prayer, or a growing disconnection from the very people we’re called to stand in the gap for. Sometimes our dreams shift from being prophetic to just chaotic. These are signs that we need to pause and allow the Lord to minister to us personally, not as intercessors, but as beloved sons and daughters.

God never asked us to carry everything. He asked us to cast our burdens on Him. We are not the Holy Spirit, and we were never meant to be overwhelmed. There’s great power in setting boundaries in the spirit, in discerning what God has truly called us to carry versus what we’ve picked up out of guilt, pressure, or over-responsibility. The truth is: obedience and peace go together. If there’s no peace in the burden, we need to revisit whether it’s from Him.

Our renewal doesn’t come just from intense prayer; it comes from intimacy. We need personal worship that has nothing to do with warfare. We need laughter, rest, nature, music, good food, sleep, and community. These things are not “unspiritual"; they are soul care, and they matter deeply. God’s intercessors must also be God’s friends. We must learn to return to the quiet place, not to perform, but simply to be loved.

In the end, a strong intercessor is not just one who can pray long and loud, but one who is rooted, rested, and steady. A person who prays from overflow, not exhaustion. The call is holy, but so is the vessel. Take care of both.

Blessings,
Dr. Adalia Hansen
Founder of Dorcas Women and Children's Fund
Vice President of World Ministries International
Email: ahansen.wmi@hotmail.com

Jonathan Hansen

item.title: The Listening Posture in Prayer: The Often-Forgotten Discipline
item.content:encoded:
In prayer, we are quick to speak, bringing requests, praise, and confession but often forget that prayer is also meant to be a conversation. One of the most neglected aspects of this dialogue is the act of listening. True communion with God requires a heart that not only speaks but also waits quietly, with the same attitude as young Samuel: “Speak, Lord, for your servant is listening" (1 Samuel 3:10).

Listening in prayer is a spiritual discipline, a posture of humility, stillness, and expectation. In our fast-paced world, silence can feel unnatural, yet Scripture reminds us, “Be still, and know that I am God" (Psalm 46:10). Stillness creates space for God’s presence to be felt and His voice to be heard.

Jesus said, “My sheep hear my voice" (John 10:27). God still speaks, through His Word, through the Holy Spirit, through the quiet nudges in our hearts but we often miss Him in the noise. Listening deepens intimacy and helps align us with His will, rather than asking Him to align with ours. It is in the stillness that we hear His guidance, receive peace, and are renewed.

Practicing a listening posture means intentionally inviting silence into our prayer time. Ask God to speak, and then wait. Journal what you sense, reflect on Scripture slowly and let it speak to your heart. As Isaiah said, “He wakens my ear to listen like one being instructed" (Isaiah 50:4).

Biblical figures like Elijah, Mary of Bethany, and Habakkuk modeled this well. Elijah heard God not in dramatic events but in a still, small voice (1 Kings 19:11–13). Mary chose to sit and listen at Jesus’ feet while others were busy, and Jesus said she chose what was better (Luke 10:39–42).

In short, listening in prayer is not passive; it is a powerful act of faith. When we quiet ourselves before God, we show reverence, trust, and a readiness to obey. May we become people who not only speak to God but who also wait, listen, and respond with a wholehearted, “Yes, Lord."

Blessings,
Dr. Adalia Hansen
Founder of Dorcas Women and Children's Fund
Vice President of World Ministries International
Email: ahansen.wmi@hotmail.com

item.title: Returning to the Ancient Paths
item.content:encoded:
Dear SOC members,

In an age of moral decline and spiritual confusion, God's people are called to return to the "ancient paths" (Jeremiah 6:16). This divine invitation urges us to seek timeless principles of righteousness, wisdom, and obedience.

Five Ancient Paths to Rediscover

  1. The Path of Holiness: Be set apart, living a life of purity in thought, speech, and actions (1 Peter 1:16).
  2. The Path of Obedience: Trust and obey God, yielding to His voice and commands (Isaiah 1:19).
  3. The Path of Faith: Live by radical faith in God's promises, trusting in His power and provision (Habakkuk 2:4).
  4. The Path of Prayer and Seeking God: Prioritize communion with God, pray without ceasing (1 Thessalonians 5:17).
  5. The Path of Love and Righteousness: Pursue justice, kindness, and humility, loving God and others with all your heart (Matthew 22:37-39).

Returning to these ancient paths is not about nostalgia, but about restoring divine order in our lives. By walking in God's ways we'll find true peace, fulfillment, and a deeper relationship with Him.

We have just returned from Kenya. We had the most amazing time of ministry including our WMI dental team joining with another dental team in a small town in Central Kenya to provide dental care for the less fortunate. There are still more trips to come so we urge you to cover us in prayer.

Blessings, Adalia Hansen

item.title: Why Does God Delay Our Prayers?
item.content:encoded:
Dear soldiers of the cross team,

Waiting for answered prayers can be a challenging and vulnerable experience, but it's essential to remember that delays are not denials. God often uses these waiting periods to refine our faith, prepare us for His blessings, and align events for His perfect timing.

Understanding the Reasons for Delayed Prayers

  1. Faith Refinement: Like Abraham, we grow spiritually through patience and trust (Habakkuk 2:3).

  2. Spiritual Preparation: Sometimes, we're not yet ready for what we're asking, and God's delay is an opportunity for growth (Ecclesiastes 3:1).

  3. Divine Timing: God's delays can lead to greater miracles, as seen in the story of Lazarus (John 11:6).

  4. Spiritual Warfare: Unseen battles can hinder our prayers, as Daniel experienced (Daniel 10:12-13).

  5. Unresolved Sin: Unrepented sin can block our prayers until we seek forgiveness (Psalm 66:18).

  6. A Better Plan: God's delays often protect us and lead to something greater, as promised in Jeremiah 29:11.

Responding to Delays with Faith and Perseverance

  1. Trust God's Wisdom: Even when we don't see results, God is working behind the scenes (Luke 1:45).

  2. Stay Persistent in Prayer: Keep praying with faith and perseverance, like the persistent widow (Luke 18:1-8).

  3. Grow Spiritually: Use the waiting time to deepen your faith and relationship with God.

  4. Surrender to His Will: Remember that God's "wait" or "no" is always for our good (Isaiah 55:8-9).

God is Never Late: if your prayer is delayed, take heart, God is still in control. His answer will come at the perfect time, and when it does, it will be far greater than what you expected. Keep trusting, keep praying, and don't lose faith.

Kindly pray for the upcoming trips to Kenya then Norway. May all meetings be divinely orchestrated for the advancement of the Kingdom.

Blessings, Adalia Hansen

item.title: His Grace is Sufficient
item.content:encoded:
Dear SOC team members,

I wanted to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude for your unwavering dedication to prayer. Your commitment to interceding on behalf of others is a precious gift, and I'm honored to be among those blessed by your prayers.

As you continue to stand in the gap, I want to remind you that God's grace is sufficient. In the midst of intense spiritual battles, exhausting prayer vigils, and weighty burdens, it's easy to feel overwhelmed. I urge you to remember that God's strength is perfected in your weakness (2 Corinthians 12:9).

His grace is the fuel that sustains you, the wind that lifts your wings, and the anchor that holds you fast. When the enemy seeks to discourage or deceive, when the journey seems long and arduous, and when your own strength falters, remember that God's grace is sufficient.

You are not alone in this sacred work. God is your Partner, your Guide, and your Reward. He sees your labor, honors your commitment, and will reward your faithfulness.

Keep praying, dear warriors. Your prayers are changing lives, shifting atmospheres, and advancing God's Kingdom.

With deepest gratitude and appreciation,

Adalia Hansen

item.title: Overcoming Distractions
item.content:encoded:
Have you ever sat down to pray, only to find your mind wandering off in a million different directions? Your to-do list, worries, and distractions seem to take over, leaving you wondering if prayer is even worth the effort.

I totally get it. Prayer can be tough. But here's the thing, it's not just about us. We're in a spiritual battle, and our enemy will stop at nothing to keep us from connecting with God.

So, why is prayer such a challenge? For one, our flesh is weak, and our minds are easily distracted. We're also tempted to prioritize our own desires over our desire for God.

But despite these challenges, we can grow in our prayer lives. Here's how:

1. Come as you are: Jesus loves you just as you are; no pretenses, no filters. He knows your heart and He's not surprised by your thoughts or feelings.

2. Remember what prayer is: Prayer isn't a formula or a system; it's a conversation with the living God. It's a relationship, and He's invited you to join Him.

3. Ask for help: Prayer is a spiritual activity, and we need God's help to make it effective. Ask Him to give you a greater desire to pray and to help you stay focused.

Growing in prayer is about building a relationship with Jesus. He's initiated the conversation, and He longs for you to know Him better. Don't give up, even when it's tough. You are loved, and you are worth it.

Continually pray that we may end well this year. We thank God for your lives and the tremendous work you are doing covering us in prayer.

Blessings and Happy Holidays!

Adalia Hansen

Dear Prayer Partner:

item.title: WHY DO THE RIGHTEOUS SUFFER?
item.content:encoded:
There are plenty of things in life we must face head-on and overcome through God's enabling power. Life's challenges demand strength and wisdom is the key to unlocking it.

As Ecclesiastes 7:19 reminds us, wisdom gives strength to the wise, surpassing the power of earthly rulers.

But in a world where the wicked may seem to prosper, we must fix our eyes on the eternal prize. There are quick wins in life and then there is the marathon. There is the vapor of this life and then the eternal life with Christ Jesus (if one chooses to follow Him).

The righteous may suffer but it's often:

To test their faith
Bring glory to God
To open people's eyes and hearts to others who are experiencing similar pain
To produce perseverance
To develop patience, trials can produce patience
To endure evil without retaliation, even if it means enduring harsh treatment for doing good. (I know, it's a bitter pill to swallow)

Through perseverance and patience, we can emerge stronger, more dependent on God, and more determined to shine His light in a dark world.

As 2 Corinthians 4:8 promises, we may be hard-pressed, but we are not crushed; perplexed, but not in despair. Our call is not to seek comfort, but to embody the Light of Christ, illuminating the world with God's glory.

We must remember that our focus should be on the marathon, not the short game. Quick wins may bring temporary satisfaction, but they pale in comparison to the eternal life that awaits us with Christ Jesus.

As Jesus taught in the Sermon on the Mount, "Blessed are the poor in spirit," not "Blessed are the rich." This truth is difficult to swallow, but it's essential to our understanding of God's kingdom.

We must continue to seek out God's truths through studying His Word and communing with Him. When the journey gets tough, we must rest in knowing that even when we don't have it figured out, God does.

God Bless, Adalia Hansen

The ministry trip to Oahu and Hawaii, speaking and ministering in seven churches, went very well. God's anointing and favor was with us. We were about $900 short of covering the expenses for this trip. If you feel God is asking you to help us, please send your best love offering.

We have postponed the trip to Kenya in December due to the Spirit of God directing us. Only $50 of the requested funds of $7,000 came in, so please consider helping so that we can go as soon as God wants us to.

Blessings, Jonathan Hansen

item.title: Relying on the Strength of the Lord
item.content:encoded:
We all face challenges and struggles in life, and sometimes we feel overwhelmed and weak. As believers, we have access to a strength that's beyond our own. Today, we're reminded that relying on the strength of the Lord is not a sign of weakness, but of wisdom.

We must recognize our own weakness and limitations. We can't do it all on our own, and our strength is limited. God's strength is limitless, and His power is beyond our comprehension. Admitting our weakness is the first step to relying on God's strength.

God's strength is perfect, and it's available to us through His Spirit. We can trust in the Lord with all our heart, casting our cares on Him and waiting on Him for renewal and strength. When we rely on God's strength, we experience courage and confidence, and we're empowered to persevere through trials.

As Philippians 4:13 reminds us, "We can do all things through Christ who strengthens us." We're not alone in our struggles, and we don't have to rely on our own strength. Let's choose to rely on the Lord's strength today and every day.

In times of uncertainty and weakness, may we turn to Psalm 28:7 which says, "The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in him, and he helps me." May we wait on the Lord, and may our strength be renewed. May we declare, "I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me."

My prayer:

We come to You Lord, acknowledging our weakness and limitations. We thank You for Your limitless strength and power. Help us to rely on You, trust in You, and wait on You for renewal and strength. In Jesus' name, Amen.

Blessings, Adalia

Dear Fellow Prayer Warrior,

item.title: Harboring Grudges Towards God
item.content:encoded:
Today, I'm sharing on a delicate topic. We've all encountered instances where God didn't align with our expectations, resulting in sentiments of disillusionment, wrath, and bitterness. It could be unanswered prayers for healing or financial relief, bereavement, distressing relationships, or unmet hopes in ministry or profession that can all contribute to these feelings.

Even biblical figures experienced similar struggles. Job, after enduring the loss of family, health, and possessions, questioned God's goodness (Job 3:25-26). David felt abandoned during Absalom's rebellion, crying out to God in Psalm 22:1-2. Asaph battled with envy and uncertainty in Psalm 73:21-26. These examples remind us that we're not alone in our doubts and fears.

However, holding grudges against God comes with dangers. Unforgiveness hinders spiritual growth, causing stagnation (Matthew 6:14-15). Resentment consumes our thoughts, leading to emotional turmoil (Psalm 38:3-4). Moreover, unforgiveness distorts our perception of God's character, creating a skewed view of His goodness and sovereignty.

To break free, we must release the grudge. This begins with acknowledging our emotions, recognizing and expressing our pain to God (Psalm 142:2). Next, we forgive God, choosing to release resentment and trust Him (Matthew 6:14-15). Finally, we refocus on God's goodness, remembering His faithfulness (Psalm 103:2-4).

Holding grudges against God is natural, but destructive. Forgiveness releases us from emotional bondage, allowing us to experience spiritual freedom, deepen our intimacy with God, and see His goodness, even in darkness. As we conclude, let's pray: "Lord, I release my grudges against You. Forgive my doubts and resentment. Show me Your goodness and faithfulness. Amen."

May God's mercy and love compel us to release our grudges and forgive, embracing a more intimate and trusting relationship with Him.

Blessings, Adalia Hansen

Dear Intercessor Partner,

Thank you for your faithful prayers, we surely need and appreciate them.

God has faithfully answered our prayers so now we can get the new computers that were so desperately needed. PTL!

item.title: STAYING POWER
item.content:encoded:
Dear Intercessors,

I recently cried out to the Lord, feeling overwhelmed by the weight of responsibilities, lofty goals, and intense spiritual warfare. I was tired and wondered how I could keep moving forward, but God's gentle whisper gave me the strength I needed: "I give you staying power."

In that moment, I realized that despite past failures and setbacks, I was still standing. I was still "riding the tide" of God's grace, and I knew I wasn't alone. The Apostle Paul's words echoed in my heart: "I press on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus" (Philippians 3:14).

Staying power is not just about perseverance, it's about being anointed by God to fulfill His purpose. It's about seeking His presence in every aspect of our lives, from our homes to our workplaces, schools, and communities.

I want to encourage you today, don't give up, even when the journey gets tough. Don't let setbacks or failures define you. Instead, draw closer to God, and ask Him to anoint you with staying power. Remember, our driving force shouldn't be earthly rewards but sharing God's love and encouraging others to succeed in Christ.

Look at Paul's life, beaten, persecuted, imprisoned, and shipwrecked, yet he remained steadfast. Joseph, betrayed and imprisoned, held onto his dreams and we can do the same.

Make up your mind to keep moving forward, fixing your eyes on the goal. Ask God to give you staying power, to press on like Paul, and to love His people with the same love He has shown you.

You are not alone in this journey. God is with you and He will give you the strength to overcome. I am praying for you as you also pray for us.

Blessings, Adalia Hansen

Dear Fellow Prayer Warriors,

item.title: Thy Kingdom Come
item.content:encoded:
Dear intercessors,

I want to share with you a struggle that I believe we all face as imperfect humans. We often find ourselves consumed by our own selfish desires, even in our prayers. We can get so caught up in our own concerns and fears that we forget to seek God's glory and kingdom.

I want to remind you of the transformative power of praying for God's kingdom to come. When we prioritize His will and glory above our own, we experience a freedom and peace that surpasses our understanding. Jesus promises that when we seek His kingdom first, our anxieties will fade and everything we need will be given.

I know it's easy to get caught up in our own needs and desires, but I encourage you to join me in praying for God's kingdom to come. Let's ask Him to align our hearts with His glorious intentions for the world. Let's pray for His kingdom to advance, for His glory to be revealed in and through us, and for Satan's kingdom to be destroyed.

When we pray with this orientation, we'll find that our own comfort and happiness will follow. We'll experience a joy that's not dependent on our circumstances, but on God's presence and power in our lives.

Let's pray together for God's kingdom to come, and that we may experience the power and glory of His presence in our lives.

We have just returned from a 3-week trip and would like to thank you for your prayers. Let us continually pray for the will of the Lord to be made manifest for this ministry and that all hindrances to be removed.

With love and prayer,

Adalia Hansen

Dear fellow warriors,

  1. Pray for the fruit of our three week road trip where we ministered in Spokane, WA., Blanchard and Oklahoma City, OK, the Dallas, TX area and Albuquerque, NM.

item.title: HIDDEN SECRETS IN GOD
item.content:encoded:
The Bible says, "...the knowledge of the secrets of the kingdom of heaven has been given to you, but not to them" (Matthew 13:11), and "…that they may know the mystery of God, namely, Christ" (Colossians 2:2). From these words, we understand that the knowledge of God's secrets are given to those who recognize and receive Christ. As members of God's family, let us press on to know Him, and He will reveal all the secrets of the kingdom of heaven.

Just as a snake and a cow can produce different outcomes from the same water, so too can the same knowledge be used for good or bad depending on the person. That's why God teaches us through parables, revealing their meaning through the Holy Spirit. God is gracious and willing to reveal hidden things, even future events (Daniel 2:22, Isaiah 45:3, Mark 4:22 and 1 Corinthians 2:10).

In Amos 3:7, God promises to reveal His plans to His servants, and in Jeremiah 33:3, He invites us to call upon Him for guidance. Seeking God's revelation through prayer can lead to creative solutions, deeper connection and clarity. However, unconfessed sin can hinder our prayers (Psalms 66:18). We must confess our sins and forgive others to maintain fellowship with God.

Jesus encourages us to persist in prayer, promising that our requests will be answered if we ask, seek, and knock (Matthew 7:7). I have experienced God's faithfulness in answering prayer and revealing His will for my life. Let us seek God's hidden things so we don't miss His divine plan.

We bless you in Jesus' name and appreciate your prayers for us. Please pray for our health, the ministry's longevity and the completion of God's work through us.

God bless you,

Adalia Hansen

Dear Prayer Partner:

We need your prayers for finances and additional partners so we can do more to warn the people of the dangers coming upon the nations.

We also need your prayers for people to give towards The Dorcas Fund so we can help widows and orphans in Kenya, Uganda, Liberia, and Tanzania in Africa; and Colombia, South America, as well as Israel.

Also, help us recruit more prayer intercessors. We need thousands of more intercessors so we can be successful in reaching the nations.

Thank you,

Jonathan Hansen

item.title: PURIFIED BY FIRE
item.content:encoded:
No one can purify their own heart. No one. We can try but we will fail. We don’t have the right material to work from to develop a pure heart. We are simply flawed. We are born with a self-will which would evidently, over time, show case itself when it's challenged or even subdued.

Many claim or desire to have a pure heart, this can only be achieved when the Lord works on our innate rebellion. To think that I have decided to have a pure heart by your own effort is setting yourself up for failure.

Proverbs 17:3 "The crucible for silver and the furnace for gold, but the LORD tests the heart."

God purifies our hearts by using external pressure. It is the tests of life that bring our selfish nature to the surface, just like silver or gold in a furnace is purified with intense heat. We find ourselves fighting the process. We want the tests to end or be lifted from us, but God uses these tests to refine us.

In the refining process, the impurities are brought to the surface and scraped off, then the process continues until the metal is pure enough to reflect an image on the surface like still water. We are reduced to a point of dependence on God to overcome whatever the intended target is for this testing.

We should all choose to allow ourselves to be melted into a trusting submission to God. After the scraping of the impurities off from us also comes healing of the wounds left in us. It is not easy but worth the work. The goal is to have a pure heart worthy to be used of God.

Let us continue to pray for ourselves to offer prayers to God from a pure heart. Strength to be an effective intercessor in this God given work. Ask the Lord on our behalf to open more doors for us to reach other nations. Pray that we get connected to the gatekeepers of those nations to be allowed access to minister to our brothers and sisters in those countries.

Pray also for the health and protection of each of the ministry’s staff. His divine protection and that we may stand against the wiles of the devil.

Adalia Hansen

Dear Intercessor:

Our trips to Kenya, East Africa and Bogota, Colombia, South America were extremely successful with tremendous outpouring of the Holy Spirit. Also, we met with not only the body of Christ but with many church and political leaders.

We have had great meetings in America as well. Look on my web page under events, both local and international, to see the many locations.

We need your constant prayers for finances and additional staff including intercessors.

We still need to go to Israel, Liberia, Uganda, Tanzania, South America again and South Korea. We also are getting ready to take a 20-plus state road trip to minister.

We need your constant prayers for support and safety as well as tremendous outpourings of the Holy Spirit.

Pray blessings upon our partners and that God will send in more staff.

Blessings and thank you,

Jonathan Hansen

item.title: THE STATE OF YOUR HEART
item.content:encoded:
We should understand that the heart is an important inner factor that plays a role in receiving the divine gifts. This is the greatest determinant of the power and glory of God in your life. God searches the heart. There is no use hiding our sins, our temptations, our rationalizations, our envy, jealousy, and bitterness. God knows it all.

As for you, my son Solomon, know the God of your father, and serve Him with a loyal heart and with a willing mind; for the Lord searches all hearts and understands all the intent of the thoughts. If you seek Him, He will be found by you; but if you forsake Him, He will cast you off forever’ (1 Chronicles 28: 9).

Do you present to God prayers that come from your heart? Not a form of prayer, but does your heart really go with the prayer you offer? People always say, "I always say my prayers."

There is as great a difference between saying prayers and really praying. Saying prayers is not praying. Why, you might as well say your prayers backwards as well as forward unless your heart goes with them!

"...He that cometh to God must believe that He is, and that he is a Rewarder of them that diligently seek him." Hebrews 11:6

Where there is that true faith in God, there is fervent prayer in the heart. How can a man pray if he does not believe in God? Until you do believe in God, you cannot pray effectively. It would be useless, I recollect that Christ has said, "God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.

"...Thy servant hath found in his heart to pray this prayer unto thee." 2 Samuel 7:27

How many men seem to begin to pray without really thinking about prayer? They rush, without preparation or thought, into this presence of God. I believe no loyal subject would seek an audience with their King to present a petition, without having first carefully prepared it. They appear to imagine that they just have to say certain words, and to stand or kneel in a certain attitude, and that is prayer.

David found his prayer in his heart. He and his heart were well acquainted if I must say. He had long been accustomed to talk with himself. There are some men, who know a thousand other people, but who do not know their own hearts. They have never talked with it, never examined it or even questioned it. They just follow its evil desires. David, when he wanted to pray, went and looked in his heart to see what he could find there, and he found in his heart to pray this prayer to God.

I remember often feeling as if I could not pray; I could get into a condition in which I am not able to pray. That is a very sad state for any child of God to be in. I examined my heart and found deep hurt that made it difficult for me to express myself to God. I went before the Lord in tears and asked Him to quicken my heart and I will call upon His Name. I do personally desire to ever possess the true spirit of prayer.

Therefore, to pray, we must enter our hearts, close the door of whatever distracts us in the external world and there we pray to God in such a way that God receives our prayer.

God bless, Adalia Hansen

Dear Fellow Prayer Warrior

Great article by Pastor Adalia Hansen! What we need is serious intercession.

We just returned from Bogota, Colombia, South America where we had a tremendous four day conference for 150 churches and their pastors. I also met with the Israeli Ambassador to Bogota, Colombia where I was able to give advice and prayer.

1. Pray for the fruit of that conference in Bogota. The New World Order is trying to topple the sovereignty of every nation. Everything I prophesied and warned about in Colombia five years ago has come to pass, including a communist President. If the church doesn't rise up in Bogota and in every nation, tyranny will sweep the nation and Christian freedoms will evaporate.

2. Pray for people in America and every nation to join Eagles Saving Nations at worldministries.org

3. Pray for more funds to come in for the Israel trip. I need much more support to make the trip and help the people devastated by the war.

4. Finally, pray for our finances. We need so much more in order to pay our bills and continue to sound the alarm.

Blessings, Dr. Jonathan Hansen

item.title: SATAN ATTACKS OUR EMOTIONS
item.content:encoded:
It's so easy to throw around theology and simple formulas and tell people, "You shouldn't feel like that! Where is your faith? God wants you to live in victory and constant joy! You should not be victimized by feelings of despair or depression." There is not a single child of God on this earth who does not, at times, experience a low period of unexplained feelings of emptiness, confusion, or fear. The longer one walks with God and the deeper one goes in Him, the more one is apt to be buffeted by Satan with such hellish weapons. Do not despair or fret if you are going through such an experience at this present time.

If you allow it to happen, feelings have a way of draining your confidence in the Lord and in yourself. Your feelings, if left unchecked and are permitted to run roughshod over your spirit, can drag you down into abject despair and fearfulness. Fear has torment. Your feelings, when in control, will lie to you like the devil himself. They are like dark, broken glasses that distort everything you look at.

Have you sometimes wondered how some crazy thoughts come to your head suddenly? You are like, “Where did that kind of thought come from?" The devil is the god of suggestive thoughts, he has mastered the act of planting crazy thoughts in people’s heads. Now, you know any thought contrary to the word of God is from the devil.

Satan works tirelessly to convince you that you’re not enough, defining you as broken and irreparable. He attacks your thoughts and uses the words of others against you. He capitalizes upon every vulnerability you have, not the least of which are unresolved emotional wounds of the past. What remains unhealed within you becomes the terrain Satan attacks to bring despair and heartache to your life. Satan wants you bitter, angry, exhausted, and hanging on by an emotional thread. If he can keep you focused on the pain, you will never discover the strength of Christ.

God created you with a longing for love, safety, belonging, security, purpose, and understanding. He wants to be the source of satisfying those desires. Unfortunately, if Satan senses an unmet longing, he will try to lure you into behaviors that promise satisfaction but, in the end, break you. Desire turned away from God brings bondage.

What you need now are action steps that will help you resist the devil and draw near to God in this great battle. Therefore submit to God. Resist the devil and he will flee from you. — James 4:7 (NKJV)

We are grateful for your prayers. This month we will be in Bogota, Colombia. Pray over the territory and bind any resistance to God’s move upon the meetings. Pray also for our safety in our going and return. Lastly, pray that the meetings will be impactful.

God bless, Adalia Hansen

I also need you to pray for a financial breakthrough not only for this trip, but for more finances to go to Israel when I return. Right now we can't go to Israel without the resources needed for this trip.

Thank you, Jonathan Hansen

item.title: Waiting on the Lord
item.content:encoded:
Waiting on the Lord may be one of the most difficult aspects of the Christian life. It is easier said than done, right? Very few of us like to wait; it reminds us we’re out of control. We don’t like waiting in traffic, in line at the supermarket, at the airport, or when our computers don’t work fast enough.

The first thing is this: waiting on the Lord requires patient trust. Will I trust that God has good reasons for telling me to wait? I don't know what they are, but will I trust that God knows what He's doing? Will I remember that things look different to God because He views things from eternity?

2 Peter 3:8 But do not ignore this one fact, beloved: with the Lord one day is like a thousand years, and a thousand years are like one day.

Often, we forget His work in us while we wait. It is as important as for what we are waiting on. Waiting means we must trust that God knows what He's doing.

Waiting, biblical waiting is not a passive waiting around for something to happen that will allow us to escape our troubles. Waiting does not mean doing nothing. Waiting is the confident, disciplined, expectant, active, and sometimes painful clinging to God. It knows that we will reap a reward. A farmer can wait all summer for his harvest because he has done his work of sowing the seed and watering the plants.

In the real issues of life, I am not just waiting around. I am waiting on God; therefore, I can trust His wisdom and His timing. I've heard it said that the person who waits on God loses no time.

Keep us in your prayers. Remember, the end of the year is soon approaching. Pray we finish strong. The Kenya Mission Trip will happen soon afterwards, pray for a safe journey and that we fulfill all we hope to accomplish.

Adalia Hansen

Dear Prayer Warrior:

In December, we will be in Oahu, HI ministering for 10 days. We need your prayers for mighty meetings. Also, in January we will be in Kenya, East Africa ministering for 17 days. Please intercede with us for tremendous moves of the Holy Spirit with signs following. Pray for safety in our travels and God’s favor. Pray for the funds to come in for the traveling and accommodations.

We also need to close this year strong financially, so pray for the bounty of God to be poured into our ministry. We need some new equipment etc., and more personnel.

Pray with us for people to join EAGLES SAVING NATIONS that I also lead. We have to have another Great Awakening if the Republic of the United States is going to survive.

Blessings, Jonathan Hansen

item.title: WHEN GOD SEEMS SILENT
item.content:encoded:
What are we to do when we pray to God, especially when we’re in desperate need and we’re met with silence? Do we conclude God didn’t hear us? Or do we think He’s not there or, worse, He doesn’t care?

I’ve prayed for something for a decade that hasn’t yet come to pass. There are months where the sting of waiting dwindles. But there are times it doesn’t, and God’s silence threatens to overwhelm me.

You can probably remember moments in your life when you felt the same way, or you might be in a season of silence right now. Experiencing somewhat of a “spiritual desertion", if we may call it that. The sense that God has forgotten us, leaving us feeling isolated and directionless.

I have begun to see that regardless of our circumstances, the Lord is never silent. There’s a song I like to listen to, it says, “in the silence, You are speaking". He might not give us immediate direction or answer the prayer how or when we’d like, but he does care about the pain.

“Wait for the Lord; be strong, and let your heart take courage; wait for the Lord!" (Psalms 27:14)

The call to wait on God is an invitation to trust and hope. It entails believing that one day, even if today is not that day, He will make all things right. My friend once told me, 'I know you are overwhelmed, but one thing you can’t fail to have is hope.'

But through faith, we can affirm God’s loving presence, even when he seems silent, and we feel deserted. “Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you" (James 4:18) is a promise God will not break, despite how we feel.

Continually pray for the ministry and the building of the foundations for Eagles Saving Nations. We also have an upcoming mission trip to Kenya next year in January. The Dorcas Fund plan is to go to a children’s orphanage. Pray for funds to come in so we can give them the best of basic needs. We are praying for you.

Warmly, Adalia Hansen

Dear Prayer Partner:

As the world knows by now, Hamas attacked Israel out of nowhere! Over 1,400 Jews killed, women raped and babies beheaded. Hundreds were taken as hostages back to Gaza.

I need more prayer partners, so pray with me that I get thousands in every nation, especially America.
